assertions.scriptName: quoted VALUES aliases remain case-distinct inside aggregates
assertions.query:
SELECT SUM("Val"), SUM("val")
						FROM (VALUES(1, 10), (2.5, 20)) v("Val", "val");

assertions.scriptName: quoted VALUES aliases remain case-distinct inside aggregates
assertions.query:
SELECT SUM(v."Val"), SUM(v."val")
						FROM (VALUES(1, 10), (2.5, 20)) v("Val", "val");

assertions.scriptName: whole-row references allow duplicate field names
numeric power supports fractional exponents
assertions.query: SELECT power(2::numeric, 0.5::numeric)::float8;

assertions.scriptName: numeric sqrt preserves PostgreSQL precision
assertions.query: SELECT sqrt(2::numeric)::text;

assertions.scriptName: numeric sqrt preserves PostgreSQL precision
numeric logarithms preserve small deltas
numeric logarithms preserve small deltas
numeric logarithms preserve small deltas
width_bucket reversed bounds underflow
assertions.query: SELECT width_bucket((-1)::float8, 10::float8, 0::float8, 5)::text;

assertions.scriptName: width_bucket reversed bounds underflow
assertions.query: SELECT width_bucket((-1)::numeric, 10::numeric, 0::numeric, 5)::text;

assertions.scriptName: CASE expression short-circuits unselected branches
assertions.query:
SELECT CASE
							WHEN id = 1 THEN 42
							ELSE 1 / (id - id)
						END
						FROM case_short_circuit_items;

assertions.scriptName: COALESCE short-circuits after first non-null argument
assertions.query:
SELECT COALESCE(value, 1 / (id - id))
						FROM coalesce_short_circuit_items;

assertions.scriptName: numeric to int4 casts round instead of truncate
array element integer casts round numeric inputs
assertions.query: SELECT array_to_string(ARRAY[37.89::int4, 1.2::int4], '_');

assertions.scriptName: bytea array cast to text array uses PostgreSQL escaping
bytea array cast to text array uses PostgreSQL escaping
array_agg over array input returns higher-dimensional array
integer primary key comparison with fractional float literal
assertions.query:
SELECT i
						FROM int_float_predicate_items
						WHERE i > 0.1 OR i >= 0.1
						ORDER BY i;

assertions.scriptName: integer primary key comparison with fractional float literal
assertions.query:
SELECT i
						FROM int_float_predicate_items
						WHERE i < 0.1
						ORDER BY i;

assertions.scriptName: float IN-list comparison with explicit float cast
INTERSECT ALL preserves duplicate counts
assertions.query:
SELECT x FROM (VALUES (1), (1), (2), (3)) AS lhs(x)
						INTERSECT ALL
						SELECT x FROM (VALUES (1), (1), (1), (3), (4)) AS rhs(x)
						ORDER BY x;

assertions.scriptName: EXCEPT ALL preserves duplicate counts
assertions.query:
SELECT x FROM (VALUES (1), (1), (1), (2), (3)) AS lhs(x)
						EXCEPT ALL
						SELECT x FROM (VALUES (1), (3), (4)) AS rhs(x)
						ORDER BY x;

assertions.scriptName: IN subquery returns boolean false for empty result
assertions.query: SELECT 1 IN (SELECT x + y FROM in_subquery_left, in_subquery_right);

assertions.scriptName: ANY subquery rejects multiple columns
assertions.query: SELECT 1 = ANY (SELECT 1, 2);

assertions.scriptName: ALL subquery rejects multiple columns
assertions.query: SELECT 1 = ALL (SELECT 1, 2);

assertions.scriptName: IN subquery rejects multiple columns
assertions.query: SELECT 1 IN (SELECT 1, 2);

assertions.scriptName: row IN subquery accepts matching multiple columns
assertions.query: SELECT ROW(1, 2) IN (SELECT 1, 2);

assertions.scriptName: row IN subquery accepts matching multiple columns
assertions.query: SELECT ROW(1, 3) IN (SELECT 1, 2);

table alias composite field selection
IN subquery cross-type equality
assertions.query: SELECT 1::int8 IN (SELECT 1::numeric);

assertions.query: SELECT 1::numeric IN (SELECT 1::int8);

assertions.scriptName: IN subquery cross-type equality
assertions.query: SELECT 1::int4 IN (SELECT 1.0::float8);

assertions.scriptName: scalar subquery equality
assertions.query:
SELECT
						(SELECT id FROM scalar_subquery_equality_items WHERE id = 2) =
							(SELECT id FROM scalar_subquery_equality_items WHERE id = 2),
						(SELECT label FROM scalar_subquery_equality_items WHERE id = 2) =
							(SELECT label FROM scalar_subquery_equality_items WHERE id = 3),
						(SELECT label FROM scalar_subquery_equality_items WHERE id = 1) =
							(SELECT label FROM scalar_subquery_equality_items WHERE id = 2);

assertions.scriptName: INSERT scalar subquery rejects multiple rows
assertions.query:
INSERT INTO scalar_subquery_insert_target
						VALUES (1, (SELECT value FROM scalar_subquery_insert_source ORDER BY value));

assertions.scriptName: INSERT scalar subquery rejects multiple rows
assertions.query: SELECT count(*) FROM scalar_subquery_insert_target;

UPDATE scalar subquery rejects multiple rows
assertions.query: SELECT value FROM scalar_subquery_update_target WHERE id = 1;

assertions.query: SELECT ROW(NULL, 4) IS DISTINCT FROM ROW(NULL, 4);

assertions.query: SELECT ROW(1, 2) < ROW(1, 3);

assertions.query: SELECT ROW(2, 1) > ROW(1, 999);

assertions.query: SELECT ROW(1, 2) <= ROW(1, 2);

assertions.scriptName: row value comparisons handle NULL fields
assertions.query: SELECT ROW(1, 2) < ROW(1, NULL);

assertions.query: SELECT ROW(1, 2, 3) < ROW(1, 3, NULL);

assertions.query: SELECT ROW(1, 2, 3) = ROW(1, NULL, 4);

assertions.scriptName: row value comparisons handle NULL fields
assertions.query: SELECT ROW(1, 2, 3) <> ROW(1, NULL, 4);

varchar comparison with blank-padded char
assertions.query: SELECT 'def'::varchar = CAST('def' AS char(6));

assertions.scriptName: varchar comparison with blank-padded char
assertions.query: SELECT 'def'::varchar < CAST('def' AS char(6));

assertions.scriptName: varchar comparison with blank-padded char
assertions.query: SELECT 'def'::varchar >= CAST('def' AS char(6));

assertions.scriptName: varchar column comparison with blank-padded char
varchar column comparison with blank-padded char
assertions.query:
SELECT v
						FROM varchar_char_compare_items
						WHERE v < CAST('def' AS char(6))
						ORDER BY v;

assertions.scriptName: varchar column comparison with blank-padded char
assertions.query:
SELECT v
						FROM varchar_char_compare_items
						WHERE v >= CAST('def' AS char(6))
						ORDER BY v;

assertions.scriptName: boolean IN predicate does not panic with boolean indexes present
assertions.query: SELECT * FROM bool_in_scan_items WHERE b IN (false);

assertions.scriptName: boolean IN predicate does not panic with boolean indexes present
assertions.query: SELECT * FROM bool_in_index_items WHERE b IN (false);

assertions.scriptName: DISTINCT ON requires matching leading ORDER BY expressions
FETCH FIRST WITH TIES includes peer rows
assertions.query:
SELECT id, score
						FROM fetch_ties_items
						ORDER BY score
						FETCH FIRST 2 ROWS WITH TIES;

assertions.scriptName: FETCH FIRST WITH TIES includes peer rows
assertions.query:
SELECT id, score
						FROM fetch_ties_items
						ORDER BY score
						OFFSET 1 ROW
						FETCH FIRST 1 ROW WITH TIES;

assertions.scriptName: FETCH FIRST WITH TIES includes peer rows
assertions.query: SELECT id FROM fetch_ties_items FETCH FIRST 1 ROW WITH TIES;

assertions.scriptName: TABLESAMPLE SYSTEM 100 returns all rows
assertions.query: SELECT count(*) FROM tablesample_items TABLESAMPLE SYSTEM (100);

assertions.scriptName: WITH RECURSIVE supports SEARCH clause
assertions.query:
WITH RECURSIVE tree(id, parent_id) AS (
							VALUES (1, NULL::INT)
							UNION ALL
							SELECT child.id, child.parent_id
							FROM (VALUES (2, 1), (3, 1)) AS child(id, parent_id)
							JOIN tree ON child.parent_id = tree.id
						) SEARCH BREADTH FIRST BY id SET bfs_order
						SELECT id FROM tree ORDER BY bfs_order;

assertions.scriptName: WITH RECURSIVE supports CYCLE clause
SELECT can project tableoid
assertions.query:
SELECT tableoid::regclass::text, id
						FROM select_tableoid_items;

assertions.scriptName: ROWS FROM pads shorter set-returning functions
multi-array unnest pads shorter arrays
generate_series supports WITH ORDINALITY
assertions.query:
SELECT v, ord
						FROM generate_series(4, 8, 2) WITH ORDINALITY AS g(v, ord);

assertions.scriptName: timestamp generate_series coerces unknown step to interval
window frame EXCLUDE CURRENT ROW excludes current row
assertions.query:
WITH items(id, v) AS (VALUES (1, 10), (2, 20), (3, 30))
						SELECT id, sum(v) OVER (
							ORDER BY id
							ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
							EXCLUDE CURRENT ROW
						)
						FROM items
						ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: window frame GROUPS computes peer-group frame
assertions.query:
WITH items(id, v) AS (VALUES (1, 10), (2, 20), (3, 30), (4, 40))
						SELECT id, sum(v) OVER (
							ORDER BY id
							GROUPS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
						)
						FROM items
						ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: window frame EXCLUDE GROUP excludes peer group
window frame EXCLUDE TIES excludes peer rows
window frame RANGE offset computes value-distance frame
cume_dist window function
nth_value window function
assertions.query:
WITH items(id, v) AS (VALUES (1, 10), (2, 20), (3, 30))
						SELECT id, nth_value(v, 2) OVER (
							ORDER BY id
							ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
						)
						FROM items
						ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: window aggregate FILTER returns NULL for empty filtered frame
assertions.query:
WITH items(id, grp, v, paid) AS (
							VALUES
								(1, 'a', 10, true),
								(2, 'a', 20, false),
								(3, 'a', 30, true),
								(4, 'b', 40, false)
						)
						SELECT id, sum(v) FILTER (WHERE paid) OVER (
							PARTITION BY grp
							ORDER BY id
							ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
						)
						FROM items
						ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: window avg FILTER returns NULL for empty filtered frame
assertions.query:
WITH items(id, grp, v, paid) AS (
							VALUES
								(1, 'a', 10, true),
								(2, 'a', 20, false),
								(3, 'b', 30, false)
						)
						SELECT id, avg(v) FILTER (WHERE paid) OVER (
							PARTITION BY grp
							ORDER BY id
							ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
						)
						FROM items
						ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: boolean aggregates can be window functions
assertions.query:
WITH items(id, grp, paid) AS (
							VALUES
								(1, 'a', true),
								(2, 'a', false),
								(3, 'b', false)
						)
						SELECT id,
							bool_or(paid) OVER (
								PARTITION BY grp
								ORDER BY id
								ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
							),
							bool_and(paid) OVER (
								PARTITION BY grp
								ORDER BY id
								ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
							)
						FROM items
						ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: array_agg can be a window function
assertions.query:
WITH items(id, grp, label) AS (
							VALUES
								(1, 'a', 'x'::text),
								(2, 'a', 'y'::text),
								(3, 'b', 'z'::text)
						)
						SELECT id,
							array_agg(label) OVER (
								PARTITION BY grp
								ORDER BY id
								ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
							)
						FROM items
						ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: lag and lead accept constant offset and default
assertions.query:
SELECT id,
							lag(v, 2, 0) OVER (ORDER BY id),
							lead(v, 2, 99) OVER (ORDER BY id)
						FROM lag_lead_constant_items
						ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: lag and lead accept dynamic row offsets
assertions.query:
SELECT id,
							lag(v, off, -1) OVER (ORDER BY id),
							lead(v, off, -2) OVER (ORDER BY id)
						FROM lag_lead_dynamic_items
						ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: substring for count syntax
assertions.query: SELECT substring('hello' for 3);

assertions.scriptName: substring similar escape syntax
assertions.query: SELECT substring('hello.' similar 'hello#.' escape '#');

assertions.scriptName: substring similar escape syntax
assertions.query: SELECT substring('Thomas' similar '%#"o_a#"_' escape '#');

assertions.scriptName: regexp_matches supports PostgreSQL regex flags
assertions.query: SELECT regexp_matches('ab', 'a b', 'x');

assertions.scriptName: regexp_matches supports PostgreSQL regex flags
assertions.query: SELECT regexp_matches(E'a\nb', '^b', 'n');

assertions.scriptName: regexp_replace replaces matches
assertions.query:
SELECT regexp_replace('foobarbaz', 'b..', 'X'),
							regexp_replace('foobarbaz', 'b..', 'X', 'g');

assertions.scriptName: regexp_split_to_array splits text
assertions.query: SELECT regexp_split_to_array('a,b,c', ',');

assertions.scriptName: regexp_like returns boolean
assertions.query: SELECT regexp_like('abc', '^a');

assertions.scriptName: regexp_count counts matches
assertions.query: SELECT regexp_count('abcabc', 'a');

assertions.scriptName: regexp_substr returns match
assertions.query: SELECT regexp_substr('abcabc', 'b.');

assertions.scriptName: regexp_instr returns position
assertions.query: SELECT regexp_instr('abcabc', 'b.');

assertions.scriptName: concat_ws skips null arguments
format dynamic width arguments
assertions.query: SELECT format('%*s|%*s', 5, 'x', -5, 'y');

assertions.scriptName: parse_ident splits qualified identifiers
string_to_table splits text into rows
assertions.query:

						FROM string_to_table('1|2|3', '|') AS g(v);

assertions.scriptName: named argument notation

SELECT make_date(year => 2026, month => 5, day => 10)::text,
